commands.review_summary:
The PR adds per-agent `agents.list[].tools.codeMode` schema/type/help support and threads active-agent code-mode resolution into catalog and model payload filtering with focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main documents agent-level `tools.codeMode`, but the strict per-agent tools schema omits `codeMode` and runtime resolution reads only top-level `tools.codeMode`.
commands.review_followup: The remaining action is contributor or maintainer runtime proof for a security-sensitive tool-surface change, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(code-mode): honor agent scoped code mode
target.body:
Fixes #83388.

## Problem
`agents.list[].tools.codeMode` was documented as a per-agent setting, but main rejected it in the strict per-agent schema. A schema-only fix would be worse than the original rejection because it would accept security/tool-boundary config without making it effective at runtime.

## Change and value
- Accept `agents.list[].tools.codeMode` in schema, exported types, labels, and help text.
- Resolve code mode from the active agent, with agent settings overriding top-level `tools.codeMode` defaults.
- Wire the active-agent result through catalog activation and model payload enforcement so agent-scoped code mode is not accepted as inert config.
- Preserve provider grouped tool payloads, such as Gemini `functionDeclarations`, while filtering code-mode payloads down to `exec`/`wait`.

## Who is affected
Operators using per-agent code-mode policy now get the documented behavior. Existing top-level `tools.codeMode` behavior is preserved. Agents without code mode continue to see the normal tool surface.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: `agents.list[].tools.codeMode` now changes the active agent's runtime tool surface instead of only passing config validation.

Real environment tested: Local source worktree `/home/captain/codex-workspace/tmp/openclaw-pr-83388` at PR head `8e4fc0405c`, rebased on `origin/main` `4c613fbfe0`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the embedded-attempt runtime harness in `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts` with top-level code mode disabled and active agent `ops` configured with `tools.codeMode=true`.

Evidence after fix: The runtime harness observes `openclawCodeModeToolSurface: true` and the outgoing model payload's visible tools are exactly `exec` and `wait`. Focused config/catalog/payload tests also show the per-agent key validates, active-agent config overrides top-level defaults, and grouped provider tool declarations are filtered without dropping `exec`/`wait`.

Observed result after fix: The runtime harness and focused tests pass on head `8e4fc0405c`; remote `pnpm check:changed` also passed on the same head.

What was not tested: No live external model/gateway API call was made; the proof is from the embedded runner/runtime payload boundary plus focused payload-wrapper coverage.

## Verification
- Focused local Vitest on head `8e4fc0405c`: `zod-schema.agent-defaults.test.ts`, `code-mode.test.ts`, `openai-stream-wrappers.test.ts`, and `attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts` passed, 116 tests total.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `oxfmt --check --threads=1 <touched files>` passed.
- Remote GCP Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` on head `8e4fc0405c` passed, selected `core, coreTests`, exit 0, lease `cbx_dbc03108e975`.
